title: Dryopteris microchlamys
taxonomy: Dryopteris microchlamys is classified as a species within the genus Dryopteris, which belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This fern is a member of the order Polypodiales and is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It is a type of wood fern that thrives in humid environments with rich soil. The classification of Dryopteris microchlamys is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its small to medium-sized fronds and distinctive leaf shape. As a member of the fern family, Dryopteris microchlamys plays an important role in the ecosystem, serving as a food source for various animals.

intro: Dryopteris microchlamys, also known as the East Himalayan shield fern, is a species of fern native to the eastern Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, boasting several health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris microchlamys include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the East Himalayan shield fern is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
